CALCULATE.description: Evaluates an expression in a modified filter context created by applying zero or more filter arguments. The most important DAX function, enabling context transition and dynamic measure recalculation.

FILTER.description: Returns a table that represents a subset of another table or expression, containing only rows where the filter expression evaluates to TRUE. Commonly used inside CALCULATE and iterator functions.

ALL.description: Returns all rows in a table or all values in a column, ignoring any filters that have been applied. Essential for calculating ratios, percentages, and grand totals against the unfiltered dataset.

ALLEXCEPT.description: Removes all context filters from the specified table except for the filters on the listed columns, enabling subtotal calculations that preserve certain dimensional groupings.

RELATED.description: Returns a related value from another table using an established relationship. Follows many-to-one relationships to retrieve a single value from the lookup table for each row context.

RELATEDTABLE.description: Returns a table of rows related to the current row context by following one-to-many relationships, equivalent to CALCULATETABLE with the related table applying row context as filter.

SUMX.description: Iterates over each row of the specified table and evaluates the expression, then returns the sum of all evaluated values. Enables row-by-row calculations before aggregation.

AVERAGEX.description: Iterates over each row of the specified table and evaluates the expression, then returns the arithmetic mean of all evaluated values. Blank values are excluded from the calculation.

COUNTROWS.description: Counts the number of rows in the specified table or table expression, including rows with blank values. Returns a whole number representing the row count of the evaluated table.

VALUES.description: Returns a one-column table of distinct values from the specified column in the current filter context, including the blank row added for referential integrity violations.

EARLIER.description: Returns the value of the specified column in an outer row context when nested row contexts exist, commonly used in calculated columns to compare current row values against outer iterations.

DIVIDE.description: Performs safe division of numerator by denominator, returning the alternate result (default BLANK) when the denominator is zero, avoiding division-by-zero errors in measures.

IF.description: Evaluates a logical condition and returns one of two values based on whether the condition is TRUE or FALSE, supporting conditional logic in calculated columns and measures.

SWITCH.description: Evaluates an expression against a list of values and returns the corresponding result, or an optional else result. More readable than nested IF statements for multi-condition logic.

SELECTEDVALUE.description: Returns the value when the filter context for the column has been filtered down to a single distinct value, otherwise returns the alternate result or BLANK for slicer-driven calculations.

DATEADD.description: Returns a table of dates shifted by the specified number of intervals (DAY, MONTH, QUARTER, YEAR) from the dates in the current filter context, enabling period-over-period time intelligence.

TOTALYTD.description: Evaluates a year-to-date total for the given expression over the specified date column, optionally with a custom fiscal year-end date and additional filters for time intelligence.

SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR.description: Returns a table containing a column of dates shifted one year back from the dates in the current filter context, commonly used within CALCULATE for year-over-year comparison measures.

RANKX.signature: RANKX(table, expression, value, order, ties) -> Integer
RANKX.description: Returns the ranking of a value in a table for the given expression, with configurable sort order (ASC or DESC) and tie-breaking behavior for SKIP or DENSE ranking.

ISBLANK.description: Returns TRUE if the specified value is BLANK, the special DAX empty state. Useful for handling missing data, null values, and referential integrity gaps in data models.
